description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The solar panels are arranged in a single, flat row, elevated above a yellow base platform that appears to serve as both a foundation and a protective housing for electrical components. The frame consists of multiple vertical and diagonal supports, likely made of steel or aluminum, providing stability and resistance to wind loads. The base platform is painted yellow with black and white markings, possibly for safety or visibility in an urban environment. No visible electrical inverter or cabling is apparent from the exterior, suggesting these components may be housed within the base. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for placement in public spaces, parking lots, or roadside installations.
description: A rectangular solar panel array mounted on a yellow base platform with a sturdy metal frame, designed for urban solar energy collection. The structure is elevated and visually marked for safety and visibility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the yellow base platform with visible markings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the supporting frame, and the elevated position of the panels above the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array and the continuation of the yellow base platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the frame supports and the base, with less visibility of the panel surface.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a steel or aluminum frame with a rectangular yellow base platform
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 panels
